JB
PART I:
INTRODUCTION TO
THE STRAIGHT LINE SYSTEM
PART 4

-- 1 of 9 --

THE OUTER WORLD OF
STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
y now it should be plain to see that nothing about The Straight Line
System exists in a vacuum. All of the parts are interrelated. All of the parts
build on previous parts. You can get good at closing by actively working some of
the parts, but to achieve greatness you have to work all the parts.
In Section 3 we began to explore The Inner World of your own brain and the first
of four key elements, which is State Management. Let’s move on to the second
key element: Vision Focus.
Future pacing, if you’ll recall, is meant to create a “movie” of your future to be
used as inspiration and motivation today.
If future pacing is a home movie then your vision is a blockbuster cinematic
production worthy of an Oscar. It’s bold, it’s bright, it’s inspiring. It’s your mission
in life and your very purpose for being. It answers the all important “why?”
questions.
Think details, think big and leave nothing out. That means asking yourself lots of
questions about every aspect of your life.
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com
B

-- 2 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
In business, that means: who are your customers and how will you market to
them? What does your sales funnel look like and will you be closing in person,
over the phone, on a website? How will you add value to your customer without
increasing your cost of doing business? How will you move customers into higher
profit products and services?
What about the atmosphere? What does your place of business look like? Details,
details, details! Make your movie so vivid that anyone can clearly see the morning
sun streaming in through the floor to ceiling windows, hear the chatter of your 24
employees, feel the rough texture of the exposed brick walls, and smell the
Peruvian coffee brewing in the lounge.
Why so much freakin’ detail? Simple. You’re programming your brain to achieve
everything in your movie. To paraphrase Napolean Hill: “Whatever the mind of
man can conceive and believe, it can achieve.”
Now let’s talk about you personally. Married? With children? Give them a leading
roles in your movie. Not married yet? Then describe your ideal spouse. Include
every aspect from physical appearance to personality type and don’t be surprised
when you actually do marry the person you’re describing.
What do you wear? What do you eat? Describe the restaurants you visit and the
home parties you host. Where do you go on vacation? How do you get there?
Describe where you live: your home and your neighborhood. And the cars you
and your spouse drive.
What about your spiritual, social and civic lives? Describe your commitment to
and involvement with each.
But why?
Ah! See, as important as all these detailed lists are to your future success, nothing
will have more of an impact on you, and what you will achieve, than the simple
question: “why?”
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 3 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
Sometimes the strategic purpose of The Straight Line System gets misunderstood.
Some people mistakenly view wealth creation as the why. Nothing could be
further from the truth. Wealth creation is the tool, the means by which you
achieve your why; your purpose in life. Those who focus solely on the money
either never achieve their objective, or they do and they feel no joy in getting
there.
Human nature is a “funny” thing. We’re more apt to do good for the people and
causes we hold close to our hearts, than we’re willing to do for ourselves. While it’s
true we can be selfish at times, given the slightest opportunity, we quickly morph
into givers.
So think deeply about your why – things like securing your children’s future,
taking care of your parents in their retirement years, advancing your religious
beliefs or a favorite charity, empowering your local community, serving your
customers like no one ever has before.
Putting the needs of others before your own is an intensely liberating experience.
It frees you from the shackles of your innate selfishness and opens the floodgates
to your innate self-motivation. All of a sudden you have a cause you can commit
to with unwavering devotion. There is no more powerful why.
And a powerful why helps you stay focused on positive outcomes when most
people will remain focused on their daily problems. It’s the difference between
moving forward and being stuck in a rut. You’re sure to have problems along the
way as well, but where your focus goes, energy flows. Use that energy to create
solutions to those problems and you’ll avoid those ruts.
Look both ways before crossing
As much as you were focused on the Good Humor man across the street as a
child, your parents instilled in you the benefits of being flexible with your vision,
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 4 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
lest you end up decorating somebody’s front bumper. That warning, although
modified a bit, still holds true today.
So your focus should be bifurcated – keeping one eye planted straight ahead on
your vision while the other scans left and right for opportunities that might pop
up along the way. This might surprise you, but far more often than not, the vision
you have today will undergo changes as time marches on. Here’s an example of
how changes do happen …
Starbucks was started by an English teacher, a history teacher and a writer. No
business background. Not even a coffee background. Sixteen years later this trio
sold the business to current CEO Howard Schultz who had been selling Xerox
copiers before joining Starbucks.
So be flexible. You might very well cross the street and get your ice cream cone.
You might just as easily step off the curb and onto a bus that’ll take you to
Candyland.
Get it in writing!
Even if you have a memory like the proverbial elephant, write your vision down in
excruciating detail. That’s because you don’t want to just remember your vision
from time to time, you want it constantly on the forefront of your mind. You
want to be able to read it, re-read it and refine it. With each rewrite, strive to
make your vision more focused, more visceral, more real. Give it life. Feel it
breathe. Listen to its heartbeat.
And don’t limit yourself to just words. Find a photo or artist’s rendering of your
dream home and plaster it on your wall. If you’re really into the details, like you
should be, get the blueprints.
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 5 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
Be bold about everything! Do not limit yourself. Act as if you have it all right now
and you will be shocked at how you become a magnet, attracting all the resources
you need to make your vision become reality.
This is not hocus pocus or B.S. This is how life works. Most people will never
achieve this level of success because they put all their focus in what’s wrong with
their lives instead of what’s right and what will be better.
Learn to share / Ask for help
Two more childhood lessons! When you can get yourself excited about your vision
then it’s time to share it with others. You might start with potential partners or
potential investors. You can’t build a business by yourself. You’ll need the expertise
of others and likely the money of still others.
When you begin looking for employees, you’ll need to share your vision with
them as well. You want to attract people who will be totally on board and who
know exactly why they’re on board. You’re the leader now and great leaders know
how to inspire greatness in others. You can do that when you’re emotionally
invested in your vision.
On the other hand, avoid being emotionally invested in your goals. Yes, there’s a
place for goals in your vision. One doesn’t replace the other. So let’s quickly run
through some rules for goals.
Goals are subservient to your vision. They’re shorter in duration and more
numerous. They help you focus on what needs to be done now, then what needs
to be done next. So look at them as milestones and the road to your vision.
Missing a goal here and there isn’t fatal and you should expect some misses as you
get your bearings. In the beginning you’re just as likely to move backward as
forward. Learn from the experience and move on.
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 6 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
Whatever you do, don’t be complacent. Don’t cower in fear. In fact, stretch your
goals a little. Then stretch them a little more. You want to have one foot firmly
planted in your comfort zone and the other dangling out there in the danger
zone. Just make sure that if you fail you can recover quickly.
DO NOT expend any emotion on these goals! That means when you achieve a
goal, don’t break open the champagne. Instead, set another goal. Keep moving
closer to your vision. Get emotionally involved in your goals and you’ll be on a
daily rollercoaster ride. And sooner or later you’re just going to puke all over
yourself.
What do you believe?
Beliefs – the third element of your Inner World. You’re capable of subscribing to
two different varieties – empowering beliefs and disempowering (or limiting)
beliefs. One is the rocket fuel that can send you to the Moon. Drink enough of
the other stuff and it’ll lay you out flat, with a guaranteed hangover in the
morning.
Problem is, if you’re like most people, you’ve been awash in limiting beliefs since
you were a child. Your parents got first crack at you with such worldly wisdom as
money doesn’t grow on trees. Then your teachers taught you to do as you’re told
and never question authority. Next, your friends came along and taught you all
the shit their parents and teachers drilled into them. Your enemies laid it on even
thicker and finally the media comes along to dull and confuse the few positive
brains cells you have left.
But as long as you’re still breathing on your own you can reverse this lifelong
trend of drip-fed limiting beliefs. See, beliefs are based on perception. It’s all about
what we think is true, not necessarily about what is demonstrably true.
So your homework for tonight and for the future is to take note of all the things
you do exceptionally well. Remember, this must be evidence based. Watch for a
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 7 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
correlation between the evidence and your certainty. Because the more certain you
are about the things you do exceptionally well (empowering beliefs), the less
certain you’ll become of all the B.S. you’ve been fed (limiting beliefs).
What’s your thermostat set at?
The fourth and final element of your Inner World is your standards, and in
particular, your financial standards. We’ll only touch upon this subject lightly
here, because to make full use of this knowledge you’d first have to have a couple
of the Modules from the Straight Line Persuasion System under your belt.
Suffice it to say your financial standards are like your thermostat – holding true to
the level of success and income you set for yourself.
All four of the elements that comprise your Inner World create your intention.
And it’s this intention that drives your success in the Outer World.
A quick review
1. 	Vision focus is the second key element of state management.
2. 	Your vision is the coming attractions of your life that incorporates your
mission and life purpose, answering the “why?” questions.
3. 	Creating your vision requires a detailed accounting of every aspect of your
life, both business and personal.
4. 	The movie you script should be so real it will affect all five of your senses
when played back.
5. 	The “why” is critical because simply desiring a lot of money or your own
business is are not sufficient motivators.
6. 	The “why” explains your deepest motivations and often they are the ones
you’ll commit to for the long haul.
© 2015 by Jordan Belfort. All rights reserved. 	www.JordanBelfort.com

-- 8 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
7. 	While focusing on your long-term vision you should also be congizant of
opportunities which may present themselves, even if they’re not directly
related to that vision.
8. 	Your goals will be milestones for your journey to your vision. You may not
hit each one, but it’s better to challenge yourself and miss a few than be
satisfied with mediocrity.
9. 	Your vision must be in writing so you can update it and review it often. Let
it be your source of inspiration.
10. You can’t do it all yourself so expect to share your vision with others with
the purpose of inspiring them.
11. Beliefs are the third key element of state management.
12. Beliefs are what we perceive as truth, whether we have evidence to support
this or not. Limiting beliefs rarely have any basis in fact. Empowering
beliefs can be easily supported by evidence.
13. Your standards are the fourth key element of state management.
14. In this respect, the issue is financial standards hold you to the level of
success and income you set for yourself.
15. Your Inner World creates your intention, which drives your success in the
Outer World.
Well, looks like we’ve come to the end of the line. Hope you enjoyed your journey
and that you picked up on the many valuable insights offered in The Fast Track
System. Put them to use, work at them and your successes will soon blossom.
If explosive results are more to your liking, you’re welcome to continue your
journey with us, this time on The Straight Line Persuasion System. Hop on board
and we’ll instantly credit you for the investment you made in the Fast Track
System.
Learn more about The Straight Line Persuasion System.
